free_text: Scattering of alpha-particles having an energy of
23.7 MeV by N-14 and O-16 nuclei
Three semiconductor detectors were used for
the registration of alpha-particles. One of them was
set under 30 degrees to the beam. It served as a
A gas target was used. The gas tank was separated
from the vacuum system of the experimental chamber
by the aluminum foil of 7 microns thickness and by the
Mylar film of 2 microns thickness. The energy waste
in the input foil and in gas under work pressure of
100 torr decreases the beam energy from 24.2 MeV
to (23.7+ -0.2) MeV. The gas was get from liquid oxygen
and nitrogen. The impurities were lower than 4%.
